bill.short_title: Veterans Benefits Continuity and Accountability Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2024

provisions.raw_text: For an additional amount for ''Compensation and Pensions'', $2,285,513,000, to remain available until expended.
notes: Supplemental appropriation under Veterans Benefits Administration heading

provisions.raw_text: For an additional amount for ''Readjustment Benefits'', $596,969,000, to remain available until expended.
notes: Supplemental appropriation under Veterans Benefits Administration heading

provisions.description: Establishes that each amount appropriated or made available by this Act is in addition to amounts otherwise appropriated for the fiscal year involved.
provisions.raw_text: Each amount appropriated or made available by this Act is in addition to amounts otherwise appropriated for the fiscal year involved.
notes: Standard supplemental boilerplate ensuring these funds are additive to regular appropriations

provisions.description: Unless otherwise provided, additional amounts appropriated by this Act shall be available under the authorities and conditions applicable to such appropriations accounts for fiscal year 2024.
provisions.raw_text: Unless otherwise provided for by this Act, the additional amounts appropriated by this Act to appropriations accounts shall be available under the auth
notes: Standard supplemental boilerplate applying existing FY2024 terms and conditions

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to submit a report within 30 days detailing corrections the Department will make to improve forecasting, data quality, and budget assumptions relating to budget submissions for Compensation and Pensions and Readjustment Benefits.
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 30 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall submit to the Committees on Appropriations and
notes: Subsection (a) — Budget formulation and forecasting report
Directed to Committees on Appropriations and Committees on Veterans Affairs of both chambers

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to submit within 60 days a report on the status of funds under Compensation and Pensions and Readjustment Benefits for fiscal years 2024, 2025, and 2026, with updates every 90 days thereafter until September 30, 2026, including information on changes to estimates or assumptions on obligations and expenditures.
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 60 days after the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall submit to the Committees on Appropriations and the Comm
notes: Proviso requires the report to be updated every 90 days with details on changes to estimates or assumptions
Covers FY2024, FY2025, and FY2026 funds in this or any other Act

provisions.description: Requires the Inspector General of the Department of Veterans Affairs to conduct a review of the circumstances and causes of the VBA FY2024 funding shortfall (announced July 19, 2024) and the VHA FY2025 funding shortfall (announced July 31, 2024), including comparisons of monthly obligations and expenditures against spend plans, accuracy of projections, changes in fund transfers or reallocations, and actions to improve accuracy and prevent future shortfalls. A report on the findings must be submitted within 180 days of enactment.
provisions.raw_text: The Inspector General of the Department of Veterans Affairs shall conduct a review of the circumstances surrounding and the underlying causes of the ann
notes: Subsection (a) establishes the IG review scope covering VBA FY2024 and VHA FY2025 shortfalls
Subsection (b) requires comparison of monthly obligations/expenditures against spend plans and analysis of projection accuracy
Subsection (c) requires review of fund transfers, reallocations, and movements within VHA including VISN, facility, VERA, and MCAS
Subsection (d) requires recommendations for improving accuracy of budget submissions under 31 U.S.C. 1105(a)
Subsection (e) requires report to Appropriations and Veterans Affairs Committees of both chambers within 180 days

flagged_issues: This is a supplemental appropriations bill to address announced VA funding shortfalls in VBA (FY2024) and VHA (FY2025)
Both appropriation accounts are no-year funds (available until expended)
